content: who wants to be a moderator? there is a lot of people here. I don't think we'll need to do much conflict resolution, but in general we'll need several admins to keep things organized and running smoothly.

Christian Williams said:

who wants to be a moderator? there is a lot of people here. I don't think we'll need to do much conflict resolution, but in general we'll need several admins to keep things organized and running smoothly.

If there's no moderator from Europe yet, I'd be up for it. I am already managing the Telegram channel, and I think it's good if we cover as much timezones as possible.

Christian Williams said:

who wants to be a moderator? there is a lot of people here. I don't think we'll need to do much conflict resolution, but in general we'll need several admins to keep things organized and running smoothly.

I could be. Could help with some server-side things as well ;).
Fabrizio, will your becoming a moderator make you more willing to engage in discussions about the scope of the code of conduct? ;-)

Nope :D I consider being a moderator as a pretty administrative task. Help people struggling if they don't know how to do things here, move topics to relevant streams if needed, calm people down if they start insulting etc.

wrt the last point, do we have something akin to a "mute" option? Mute is very useful. Whereas "ban" just kicks you out of the server, "mute" allows you to read, but not to write. It's a very effective tool when people become toxic, you just mute them for a couple of hours and things go back to normal. :)

Guess I'll volunteer myself as moderator, depending on how many are needed. (Just like real life democracy, I suspect that restricting to only people who volunteer themselves is terrible but unavoidable)
I'll also agree with Fab on the last 2 points. An admin is mostly tech support and occasionally executioner, but not judge or jury. I want no more or less weight than anybody else in deciding policy
And also a mute option would be really amazing
Yes, I guess we could not resort to ban altogether and jut perma-mute someone if it comes to that
Hm. It looks like there isn't any option for muting.
This is strange, this sort of stuff has been around since IRC services addons were invented in the late 90s
I think a lot of these omissions in Zulip come from it being an open source project that doesn't have many full-time developers, so they have limited bandwidth to add new features

This is very possible. But usually all these room chat apps are inspired by IRC, so I'd expect these functionalities to be implemented from the start. Maybe their starting point was completely different?
Ok, so there's this other thing which is very useful: It turns out you can create videocalls on the fly
Click to join video call

There's just the camera button below the field where you write. You click it and it generates a meeting room for you. We should try it, this could make life way easier when it comes to coordinate seminars/etc with the activity carried on here

that will be really useful. I don't know how zoom and jitsi compare, but we can configure that as well.
so it generates a new room whenever we want? and that link continues to point to that room indefinitely?
I don't know if it's indefinite, but you can just press this magic button and...
POOF! Call created
We could actually try this thing for the meeting
Worste thing that can happen it sucks and then we switch to zoom :slight_smile:
awesome.
it says firefox is not fully supported, but it seems to work fine.
Is there a way to close a topic? For instance, there was this "hello all" topic on general. I moved the message in there to "indroduce yourself!", but now I'd like to close the topic altogether
I think if you move all messages in a topic, the topic is no longer visible
Yeah, that looks to be the only way.
No, you can. on the Streams sidebar, when you click on a stream and it displays the topics, each one has a little dropdown. Click on one and it has the option to narrow, mute, mark, or delete.
mute is personal and delete removes messages
I read @Fabrizio Genovese's message as indicating they wanted to lock the topic, which isn't possible

Nathanael Arkor said:

mute is personal and delete removes messages

this :slight_smile:
As long as empy topics aren't visible, I guess we're ok
topics are more like a filter than a concrete entity
oh, I see. no, you can't close a topic. I don't really think that's necessary.
FYI, only admins can "Delete all messages in TOPIC", and it's only a convenience feature over the ability to delete messages individually.

Christian Williams said:

who wants to be a moderator? there is a lot of people here. I don't think we'll need to do much conflict resolution, but in general we'll need several admins to keep things organized and running smoothly.

:raising_hand:

If/when I become a moderator, I'll need to know what streams I should be moderating, since there's no way I can keep track of them all, and I'm obviously biased towards working on streams that I contribute to, but I don't want there to be unmoderated streams.

Alright, I made you an admin. Please let us know if you think about any changes.

As for moderating a specific thread, you can choose whichever you want. Things will be gradually reorganized, but none of the existing ones will be deleted.
